headline: Kirkwood Pulse • April 28, 2026
overview: Kirkwood City Council approved an $8 million certificate of participation to fund renovations and relocate public works to the former A-Mrazek property, addressing infrastructure needs without raising taxes. This move aims to facilitate the sale of the old public works site and support local development, impacting city finances and services.

items.title: Kirkwood: $8 Million For Public Works Refit Passes 4-1
items.excerpt: Kirkwood will invest another $8 million into a new home for its public works departments after a final — albeit split — vote of the city council last week. Meeting in regular session for the last time before three new council members take office, the Kirkwood City Council on April 16 — in a 4-1 vote — approved the issuance
